Grad program shortlist comparison. Fit notes for the coral recovery and Carolina coast angle. Distance from Wilmington and the family situation matters as much as the ranking here.
How each one lines up with coral recovery work

In state and close, UNCW, Duke Beaufort, UNC IMS Morehead City, ECU. Strong coral programs but far, Miami Rosenstiel, Hawaii HIMB, FAU. Cold water and no coral focus, MIT-WHOI, Oregon State, UCSB. Whitfield is pushing Duke and Miami hardest.
Finalize the four to five I actually apply to and get the statements moving
Grad school campus visit planning. Which programs to visit and the travel that fits the 4Runner and the dive shop shifts.
Drive to Duke Beaufort and UNC Morehead City in one loop. Fly for Miami and Hawaii only if they invite for an interview weekend.
Confirm which visits are worth the airfare before booking anything
Grad program tracker, funding and deadlines. Figures below were copied from program pages back in September and I have not re-checked them all against the admissions emails.
Standings as of my September pass

UNC Wilmington MS, application deadline Feb 15, assistantship covers full tuition plus a 22,500 stipend, GRE not required. Duke PhD Nicholas School, deadline Jan 15, guaranteed 5 year funding at 34,800, GRE optional. UNC Chapel Hill IMS MS, deadline Jan 5, TA line at 19,000, GRE required. College of Charleston Grice MS, deadline Jan 10, partial tuition waiver only no stipend, GRE required.

University of Miami Rosenstiel PhD, deadline Jan 2, fellowship at 32,000, GRE optional. MIT-WHOI PhD, deadline Dec 15, full funding at 41,000, GRE not required. UCSB Bren MS, deadline Jan 15, no guaranteed funding, GRE waived. Hawaii Manoa HIMB PhD, deadline Jan 1, RA at 28,000, GRE required. Oregon State Hatfield MS, deadline Feb 1, TA at 26,000. USF Marine Science PhD, deadline Dec 1, stipend 30,000. UGA Skidaway MS, deadline Jan 15, partial waiver. ECU MS, deadline Feb 1, TA at 21,000. Texas A&M Galveston PhD, deadline Jan 8, RA at 29,000. FAU coral restoration MS, deadline Jan 20, stipend 24,000.
